Long ago, before the mountains had voices, lived a spirit named Aethel. Aethel was the keeper of memories, weaving the laughter and tears of mortals into silver threads. She resided in a crystalline cave at the world’s highest peak, storing these threads in glowing jars.

One evening, a boastful storm god named Vael challenged Aethel. He claimed his thunder was the only sound worthy of eternity. To prove his point, he shattered Aethel’s jars with a mighty crack of lightning. The silver threads of memory scattered into the winds, drifting across canyons and valleys.

Heartbroken, Aethel begged the earth to help her catch the lost sounds. The mountains took pity on her. Their stone walls hardened, catching the fragments of sound as they flew past, bouncing them back into the air so they would never truly disappear.

Vael’s thunder was caught too, doomed to repeat endlessly, a hollow mockery of its original power. But Aethel’s treasures—the calls of lost travelers, the songs of shepherds, and the shouts of children—were preserved. Today, when you call out to the mountains, it is Aethel’s magic returning your voice, weaving your fleeting moment into the eternal tapestry of the world.

Desperate to bring them comfort, Lumis crept into the forge of the Sun God while he slept and stole a single, glowing ember.

However, the ember was too hot to carry. As Lumis fled across the night sky, it burned his hands, causing him to fumble. The ember shattered into thousands of tiny, glowing fragments that rained down upon the earth. Panicked, Lumis transformed the falling sparks into tiny winged insects so they wouldn't burn the forests below.

These creatures, born of stolen sunlight, scattered across the meadows and woods. The Sun God, waking to find his ember missing, demanded it back. But seeing the joy the gentle, blinking lights brought to the mortals, he relented, allowing the insects to keep their twilight glow.

To this day, the fireflies emerge only at dusk, carrying the fragments of the stolen sun, a reminder of Lumis’s gift and the light that conquered the ancient dark.
